fitScoreNote: Strong on agentic AI systems and data flywheels; structural gap is no formal PM title at a consumer PLG company. The exercise submission is the X-factor.
jobDescriptionText:
Role: Product Manager (Builder)
Department: Product Management

Perplexity is looking for product managers to join a highly leveraged, small team for building new products that innovate to accelerate human productivity. In 2026, we launched Computer, the defining product for the new era of agentic AI. We've scaled beyond the millions of people using Perplexity every day for research, shopping, investing and curiosity into a new paradigm of using AI to transform knowledge into action.

What you'll do
- Anticipate opportunities for innovation and value in enterprise industries
- Envision new experiences and deliver a long-term vision
- Understanding of levers for product-led growth and retention
- Work closely with engineering and design to align product expectations and capabilities
- Work with research to evaluate and steer nondeterministic models into the high value outcomes for users
- Work with data and user research to understand quantitative and qualitative data
- Have conviction to make difficult product decisions in the face of uncertainty

Qualifications
- Strong experience with product management and leadership
- Strong experience working with data and metrics
- Experience with productivity and knowledge work products
- Experience with prototyping and data visualization
- Experience with building data driven flywheels for iterative improvement
- Thrives in a small, agile team: has initiative and desire for ownership
- 4+ years of product management experience

Note: In the application, please highlight which industry you are most interested in innovating.

strengths.title: Production agentic AI systems with real financial stakes
strengths.description: 943 commits and 500 pull requests in 7 months at Smarter Technologies while managing $31.8M in active pipeline with two people. The platform enabling that scale was entirely self-built: 22 skills, 5 parallel agents, 57 Python integration scripts. Not a PM who hands specs to engineers. The person who builds the thing, then makes the next call about what to build.

strengths.title: Nondeterminism-as-a-design-parameter philosophy
strengths.description: At Thoughtful I built a hybrid-RAG eligibility system that hit 95% accuracy within two days on a live billing workflow. The architecture was designed to converge toward determinism: as human-verified outputs accumulated, future identical cases bypassed the model entirely. Nondeterminism is a design parameter, not a property to tolerate. You decide where it is acceptable and engineer the boundary.

strengths.title: Data flywheel with hooks-based usage tracking
strengths.description: Built a hooks-based usage tracking system on top of the agent platform deployed to my sales team. Every skill invocation was logged. On 15-20% of usages the system prompted for feedback: thumbs up, down, or a written note. A cluster of thumbs-down on the pricing skill with notes about missing EHR context told me exactly what to fix. Same instinct at Action Network: built the tracking pipeline that identified the highest-LTV funnel and created Value Clicks as the north star metric.

strengths.title: Exercise submission: pitched Perplexity on building the healthcare agent data layer
strengths.description: Did not teach a hobby topic. Used Perplexity to argue that Perplexity should build the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer, backed by investor and partner memos. Spent two years building exactly the systems that make that argument non-obvious. The thesis is not something I read about. It is something I have been building toward.

weaknesses.title: No formal PM title at a consumer product company
weaknesses.description: PM title is short in history. PLG at Perplexity's scale (self-serve SaaS growth mechanics) is undocumented experience. All AI product work is enterprise automation.
weaknesses.mitigation: Promoted to Lead TPM at Thoughtful in 3 months because I was already making build/don't-build calls before the title reflected it. During that window, implementations drove 63% of net new ARR during a 3x growth year. The title was the formalization. The work was already happening.

weaknesses.title: Consumer PLG at scale
weaknesses.description: Consumer product analytics work was at Action Network and FanDuel: built the LTV models and identified the funnel, but was not the PM making the feature calls.
weaknesses.mitigation: Would be learning Perplexity's specific growth mechanics while contributing on the agentic AI and enterprise sides where I have genuine depth.

referralBlurb: I'm referring Dan Mathieson for the Product Manager, Builder role. Dan is the unusual case of someone who both builds production AI systems and makes product decisions about them. At Smarter Technologies he shipped 943 commits in 7 months building a 22-skill agentic platform that ran live on $31.8M in pipeline, with hooks-based usage tracking and a data flywheel for iterative improvement. For the application exercise, he used Perplexity itself to argue that Perplexity should build the MCP-native healthcare agent data layer, backed by investor memos and two years of domain work. That kind of conviction and initiative is what the role is looking for.

projects.name: Notion Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Knowledge work tool that turns meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence, the same core problem Perplexity solves for knowledge workers.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production hybrid-RAG system designed for convergent determinism, directly relevant to Perplexity's core challenge of making nondeterministic AI reliably useful.
